edges.description: Loss of TP53-associated genome surveillance permits accumulation of chromosome-scale alterations
Genome-scale alterations are linked to Wnt pathway activation in CPC models and human tumors
Copy-number imbalance supports a highly proliferative transcriptional state
Wnt hyperactivation reduces mature epithelial differentiation and favors tumorigenic state
Dedifferentiated epithelial cells adopt a more proliferative tumor phenotype
Intraventricular tumor growth obstructs cerebrospinal fluid pathways and contributes to hydrocephalus.
Tumor growth with hydrocephalus and raised intracranial pressure causes headache.
Raised intracranial pressure from tumor-associated hydrocephalus contributes to vomiting.
Large supratentorial intraventricular tumors can irritate adjacent brain tissue and produce seizures.

nodes.description: Compared with papilloma, CPC shows transcriptional upregulation of cell-cycle genes and a more invasive progression-associated program. This state supports rapid growth and dissemination-prone behavior.

evidence.reference_title: Comprehensive multiomics analysis reveals distinct differences between pediatric choroid plexus papilloma and carcinoma.
evidence.snippet: Differential gene expression analysis uncovered a significant overexpression of genes related to cell cycle regulation and epithelial-mesenchymal transition pathways in CPC compared to CPP.

nodes.description: CPC genomes are dominated by broad copy-number change rather than numerous recurrent point mutations. Whole-chromosome gains and losses, multiple monosomies, and in a subset complex rearrangements or chromothripsis, are characteristic.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular heterogeneity of pediatric choroid plexus carcinomas determines the distinctions in clinical course and prognosis.
evidence.snippet: Unbalanced whole-chromosome aberrations, notably multiple monosomies, were highly typical.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular heterogeneity of pediatric choroid plexus carcinomas determines the distinctions in clinical course and prognosis.
evidence.snippet: In 7 tumors, chromosome losses were combined with complex genomic rearrangements: segmental gains and losses or signs of chromothripsis.

nodes.description: Headache is a common presenting symptom of CPC, typically reflecting raised intracranial pressure from intraventricular tumor growth and hydrocephalus.

evidence.reference_title: Asymptomatic choroid plexus carcinoma in an infant: Report of one case.
evidence.snippet: The Main symptoms of CPC include nausea, vomiting, headache, irritability, blurred vision, and seizures.

nodes.description: Hydrocephalus is common at presentation because the tumor obstructs CSF pathways and may also contribute to CSF hypersecretion.

evidence.reference_title: Persistence of communicating hydrocephalus post choroid plexus tumor resection: Case reports and review of literature.
evidence.snippet: Hydrocephalus is the most common presentation of choroid plexus tumors; it is thought to be caused either by mass effect obstructing the cerebrospinal fluid pathways or secretory properties of the tumor.

nodes.description: CPC cells lose features of mature choroid plexus epithelium. Experimental Wnt hyperactivation reduces differentiation of mature epithelial cells and supports oncogenic transformation of choroid plexus organoids.

evidence.reference_title: Activation of Wnt/β-catenin signaling is critical for the tumorigenesis of choroid plexus.
evidence.snippet: Increased activation of the Wnt/β-catenin pathway in ChP organoids, through treatment with a potent GSK3β inhibitor, reduced the differentiation of mature ChP epithelial cells.

nodes.description: Seizures can accompany CPC presentation, particularly with large supratentorial intraventricular tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Asymptomatic choroid plexus carcinoma in an infant: Report of one case.
evidence.snippet: The Main symptoms of CPC include nausea, vomiting, headache, irritability, blurred vision, and seizures.

nodes.description: TP53 alteration is the dominant recurrent genetic lesion in CPC. Germline or somatic loss of TP53 function removes a major checkpoint barrier to malignant transformation of choroid plexus epithelial cells.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular heterogeneity of pediatric choroid plexus carcinomas determines the distinctions in clinical course and prognosis.
evidence.snippet: All studied CPCs had smooth mutational profiles with the only recurrent event being TP53 variants, either germline or somatic, encountered in 13 cases.

nodes.description: Vomiting is a common presenting symptom of raised intracranial pressure in CPC.

evidence.reference_title: Asymptomatic choroid plexus carcinoma in an infant: Report of one case.
evidence.snippet: The Main symptoms of CPC include nausea, vomiting, headache, irritability, blurred vision, and seizures.

nodes.description: Wnt/beta-catenin signaling is activated in human choroid plexus tumors and functionally required for survival of CPT-derived cells. Experimental pathway activation is sufficient to drive oncogenic behavior in choroid plexus models.

evidence.reference_title: Activation of Wnt/β-catenin signaling is critical for the tumorigenesis of choroid plexus.
evidence.snippet: We discovered that Wnt/β-catenin signaling is activated in human CPTs, likely as a consequence of large-scale chromosomal instability events of the CPT genomes.

evidence.reference_title: Activation of Wnt/β-catenin signaling is critical for the tumorigenesis of choroid plexus.
evidence.snippet: We demonstrated that CPT-derived cells depend on autocrine Wnt/β-catenin signaling for survival.
